headline: UPS (UPS) Stock Looks Undervalued Despite Its 33% Five Year Slide
summary: United Parcel Service stock has staged a rebound over the past year, yet after a 32.9% decline over five years and fresh scrutiny of its large U.S. Postal Service air cargo contract, the current market price still sits at a discount to an intrinsic value estimate based on a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) approach. United Parcel Service shares are down 32.9% over five years, which means long term holders are still sitting on material losses despite the more recent recovery. On the upside, efforts...

headline: New Ownership of Pak Mail in San Marcos, Texas Brings Fresh Energy to the Business--Building Upon More than 20 Years of Operation
summary: Annex Brands, Inc., a leading franchisor in the packing, shipping, and office services industries, is pleased to announce the successful transfer of ownership of an existing Pak Mail location in San Marcos, TX to new owner, Zach Shifrin. This transition to new ownership ensures that the community will benefit from the essential shipping and office services provided by this Pak Mail location since 2003.

headline: UPS (NYSE:UPS) Settles Former Driver Racial Discrimination Case After Lengthy Legal Fight
summary: United Parcel Service settled a high profile racial discrimination lawsuit involving a former UPS driver after a multi year legal battle. The case, which initially produced a large jury award before most punitive damages were later vacated on appeal, has now been dismissed following the settlement. The resolution ends a lengthy federal dispute that raised questions about UPS's employment practices, legal risks, and reputation management. The settlement arrives as United Parcel Service,...
